subject: Run all components in one namespace
commit_message:
Run all components in one namespace

Change-Id: I5fcd4d577c1fda4de27842807c7cf7a5d372756e
Issue-ID: OOM-722
Signed-off-by: BorislavG <Borislav.Glozman@amdocs.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

(4 comments)

I like where this is trying to go.  Definitely would like to get rid of multiple namespaces.

What level of validation was done on this patch?  
How is robot health?  
Can you onboard a VNF and distribute it?
Can you deploy it using SO?

This draft review is definitely not ready for reviewing. It is just to save my work. I will notify when it will be done.

(1 comment)

Hi Borislav,

all json configuration files and script inside consul are mapped using config map. so can we use tpl "{{ .Values.nsPrefix }} to replace url/ pod namespace in run time..

Thanks,
Mayank Gupta

Patch Set 13:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Hi Borislav,
 > 
 > all json configuration files and script inside consul are mapped
 > using config map. so can we use tpl "{{ .Values.nsPrefix }} to
 > replace url/ pod namespace in run time..
 > 
 > Thanks,
 > Mayank Gupta

Once that review will be merged, I will incorporate those changes.
Uploaded patch set 14.

Patch Set 13:

(39 comments)

WOW, I think it took me 30 mn to review... great work Borislav. Few things:

- I think all the service and deployment should be prefixed with the application name. It seems you've done it for the deployment, but we should also do it for the services. That way, we can look for a particular service/dep/pod using app-*
- The filebeat.yml file that live in every app can now live at one place only, under the log, and be mounted from there in any app.
- The placeholder `onap-namespace` in the config should be made more obvious that it is a placeholder, I suggess `{{ ONAP_NAMESPACE }}` else it's confusing as it is a real namespace.
Patch Set 14: Code-Review-1
Uploaded patch set 15.

I implemented most of them.
Testing.
